items.q: What three notes form a major triad in root position?
items.why: Students think 'three notes = a triad'. Triads are specific: stacked thirds from the root. C major = C, E, G (skipping D and F). Consecutive scale notes are clusters, not triads.

items.q: What happens in the recapitulation of sonata form?
items.correct: Both subjects return, but the second subject is now in the tonic key.
items.why: Students miss the KEY RESOLUTION. The exposition put subject 2 in a contrasting key; the recapitulation brings it back to the tonic, resolving the tonal tension - this is the dramatic point of sonata form.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.
Correct answer: ABA. “AB” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which section in pop repeats with same lyrics?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Chorus. “Verse” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many distinct sections in binary form?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
